key_insight: Laser welding depth strongly influences lap-joint quality and strength, with the study building a FEM-based model to estimate joint performance from welding parameters.
engineering_relevance: The work supports parameter selection and process planning for laser-welded lap joints by linking penetration depth to mechanical strength and validating simulations against experiments.
method: Built lap joints from X5CrNi18-10 austenitic steel plates (100 × 200 × 2 mm).
Performed experimental welding at two penetration depths: full and partial penetration.
Developed Abaqus FEM discrete models and compared simulation results with mechanical and macroscopic test data.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental strength measurements, macroscopic examinations, and validation of numerical simulations against measured joint properties.
watch_item: The model is validated on a specific material and geometry, so broader use may require recalibration for other alloys, thicknesses, or joint designs.
paper_title: Analysis of the Influence of Laser Welding Parameters on the Quality of the Lap Joint
